content.problemStatement:
A permutation of length NNN is an array of NNN integers A=[A1,A2,…,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, \dots, A_N]A=[A1​,A2​,…,AN​] such that every integer from 111 to NNN appears in it exactly once.

We define a function over a permutation as follows:
F(A)=(A1∗A2)+(A2∗A3)+⋯+(AN−2∗AN−1)+(AN−1∗AN)F(A) = (A_1 * A_2) + (A_2 * A_3) + \cdots + (A_{N-2} * A_{N-1}) + (A_{N-1}*A_N)F(A)=(A1​∗A2​)+(A2​∗A3​)+⋯+(AN−2​∗AN−1​)+(AN−1​∗AN​)

You are given an integer NNN. What is the expected value of the function FFF over all possible permutations of length NNN?

The expected value of the function can be represented as a fraction of the form PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​. You are required to print P⋅Q−1(mod1 000 000 007)P \cdot Q^{-1} \pmod{1 \, 000 \, 000 \, 007}P⋅Q−1(mod1000000007).


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a single line the expected value of the function modulo 1 000 000 0071 \, 000 \, 000 \, 0071000000007.
constraints: All calculations are to be performed modulo MOD = 1 000 000 007 (a prime).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (2 ≤ N ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the expected value of F over all permutations of length N, represented as P·Q⁻¹ modulo 1 000 000 007, where the expectation equals the fraction P/Q in lowest terms.

solution.approach:
For a random permutation, each ordered adjacent pair (A_i, A_{i+1}) is an ordered pair of distinct numbers from 1..N, uniformly among N·(N‑1) possibilities. The expected product of one such pair is (Σ_{x≠y} x·y) / (N·(N‑1)). Using Σ_{x≠y} x·y = (Σ x)^2 – Σ x^2, we get:
  S = N(N+1)/2,   S2 = N(N+1)(2N+1)/6.
  Expected total F = (N‑1)·(S^2 – S2) / (N·(N‑1)) = (S^2 – S2) / N.
Thus the answer is ((S^2 – S2) mod MOD) * inv(N) mod MOD, where inv(N) = N^{MOD‑2} mod MOD (Fermat). All operations are performed modulo MOD. The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case after O(log MOD) for the modular inverse.
Edge cases: N=2 gives integer answer 2; for many N the result is fractional, handled by modular inverse.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·log MOD) – each test case needs a fast exponentiation for the modular inverse.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few 64‑bit variables are stored.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys
MOD = 1_000_000_007

def mod_pow(a, e):
    r = 1
    while e:
        if e & 1:
            r = r * a % MOD
        a = a * a % MOD
        e >>= 1
    return r

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    inv2 = (MOD + 1) // 2  # 500000004
    inv6 = mod_pow(6, MOD - 2)
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        n_mod = N % MOD
        n1 = (N + 1) % MOD
        two_n1 = ((2 % MOD) * n_mod % MOD + 1) % MOD
        S = n_mod * n1 % MOD * inv2 % MOD          # N(N+1)/2
        S2 = n_mod * n1 % MOD * two_n1 % MOD * inv6 % MOD  # N(N+1)(2N+1)/6
        numerator = (S * S - S2) % MOD
        ans = numerator * mod_pow(n_mod, MOD - 2) % MOD
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
